name: Drake Bank
description_short: Drake Bank is an independent community bank in St. Paul, Minnesota, chartered in 2002, offering personal and business banking with a focus on local relationships and customized financial solutions.
description_long:
Drake Bank opened in May 2002 and was the first new bank chartered in St. Paul in 40 years. As an independent, locally-owned community bank, Drake Bank positions itself around relationship-driven banking rather than transactional services. The bank operates with a mission to build authentic relationships with clients and contribute to community development, particularly by supporting small business growth.

Drake Bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services including personal and business checking and savings accounts, deposit products with published rates, personal and business loans, mortgages for home purchase and refinance, treasury management services for businesses, online banking platforms for both personal and business customers, and credit card products through their Elan Credit Card program. They maintain physical branch locations with ATM networks and provide secure email and file sharing capabilities for clients.

The bank distinguishes itself through a stated commitment to personalized service and community focus. Their marketing emphasizes a three-step process of listening to client needs, customizing solutions, and providing hands-on support. Customer reviews highlight responsive mortgage teams, knowledgeable staff, fair loan terms, and strong community commitment to small business support. The bank appears to compete on service quality and local expertise rather than product innovation or aggressive pricing.

As a regional community bank, Drake Bank serves a limited geographic area (St. Paul/Minnesota focus) and likely cannot compete with national banks on product breadth, technology platforms, or interest rates. Customers should expect higher fees and potentially less competitive rates compared to larger institutions or online-only banks. The strength lies in personal banking relationships and local decision-making, which appeals to small business owners and customers who prioritize service over price optimization. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Independent community bank with local decision-making authority and deep St. Paul roots since 2002
Personalized service model with relationship managers who customize solutions rather than offering one-size-fits-all products
Strong reputation for small business support and actively helping entrepreneurs build their vision
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, mortgages, treasury management, and business banking
Responsive mortgage team noted for educating clients on refinance options and efficient execution
Staff continuity and familiarity—customers report knowing bank employees personally
Multiple account login portals and online banking options for both personal and business customers
cons: Geographic limitation to St. Paul/Minnesota region means no national or online-only accessibility
As a smaller community bank, likely cannot compete on interest rates or deposit yields versus larger or online-only banks
Limited product innovation compared to fintech competitors or major national banks
No specific information provided about fees, minimum account balances, or pricing competitiveness
Smaller institution may have fewer ATM locations and less robust mobile app functionality than large national banks

contact.address: 60 Plato Blvd E, Saint Paul, MN 55107

best_for: Small business owners in the St. Paul area seeking a local banker who understands their business and community
Homebuyers and refinancers who value educational guidance and personalized mortgage support
Customers who prioritize personal relationships and local decision-making over competitive rates or extensive branch networks
Community-minded depositors who want to support local business growth through their banking choices
diagnosis: Drake Bank is best for small business owners and homebuyers in the St. Paul area who value personalized service, local relationships, and community impact over competitive pricing or extensive geographic reach. The main caveat is that as a regional community bank, they cannot compete with national banks on rates, fees, or technology platforms, and customers outside Minnesota cannot access their services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 60 Plato Blvd E, Saint Paul, MN 55107
